description_long: This object is a large, upright pipe organ with a wooden exterior and a prominent array of metal pipes of varying lengths arranged in a symmetrical pattern. The central section features a keyboard console with multiple manuals and an array of control stops on either side, all housed in a dark wood finish. The pipes are set against a textured, dark background, and the tallest pipes are positioned at the outer edges, with shorter pipes forming a stepped pattern toward the center. The organ's casework is robust and angular, with a deep, recessed top section and side panels that frame the instrument. The lower section beneath the pipes is solid wood, providing structural support and visual balance. This imposing instrument is designed for stationary installation, likely in a concert hall, church, or other public venue.
description: A large, upright pipe organ with a wooden case, multiple metal pipes, and a central keyboard console with control stops. The pipes are arranged in a stepped, symmetrical pattern against a dark, textured background.
description_view_0: Side view showing the tall wooden case and a partial view of the metal pipes arranged in a stepped pattern.
description_view_1: Frontal view displaying the full array of metal pipes, the central keyboard console, and the control stops on either side.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with the stepped metal pipes and wooden case visible.
description_view_3: Rear or back view showing the wooden case with no visible pipes or keyboard console.
